title: Error when pull image after set storage to ntfs folder
body:
### Issue Description

The same pull image command works by default settings (podman pull nvidia/cuda:11.8.0-cudnn8-runtime-ubuntu22.04). However, if I configure the storage location to a mounted ntfs drive, it generate error: 
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:aece8493d3972efa43bfd4ee3cdba659c0f787f8f59c82fb3e48c87cbb22a12e": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): open /var/lib/dpkg/info/gcc-12-base:amd64.list: invalid argument

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. mount an ntfs drive to ubuntu
2. set ~/.config/containers/storage.conf based on https://docs.oracle.com/en/operating-systems/oracle-linux/podman/podman-ConfiguringStorageforPodman.html#configuring-podman-storage. Change runroot and graphroot to folders on the ntfs drive.
3.podman pull nvidia/cuda:11.8.0-cudnn8-runtime-ubuntu22.04


### Describe the results you received

stopped pulling at one of the blobs.

### Describe the results you expected

Pull all blobs successfully

comments.body:
please run the following command and attach here the generated `strace.log` file:

`podman unshare strace -f -v -s 1000 -o strace.log podman pull nvidia/cuda:11.8.0-cudnn8-runtime-ubuntu22.04`
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
closing the issue, got no feedback after my last comment
